notes_md:
**đút** = to feed someone by putting food directly into their mouth (usually kids, elderly, sick people).

It’s used when one person actively gives food to another, not just “serving” but physically putting it in.  

It also very commonly means **to put/stick something into something**.  

Related phrase: **đút lót** = to bribe (literally “slip something in”), a very common fixed expression.
exampleSentences: Mỗi sáng, bà thường đút cháo cho em bé ăn.
Chị giúp mẹ đút cơm cho ông ngoại khi ông bị ốm.
Anh sẽ đút thức ăn cho mèo khi đi làm về.
notes:
đút = to feed someone by putting food directly into their mouth (usually kids, elderly, sick people).

It’s used when one person actively gives food to another, not just “serving” but physically putting it in.  

It also very commonly means to put/stick something into something.  

Related phrase: đút lót = to bribe (literally “slip something in”), a very common fixed expression.
exampleSentencesTranslated: Every morning, grandma usually feed porridge to the baby.
She helps mom feed rice to grandpa when he is sick.
He will feed the cat when he comes home from work.
